source_jobs.title: Sr Engineering Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about


THE ROLE

As our Senior Engineering Manager, you will be the linchpin between rapid execution and sustainable scaling. We are growing quickly, and we need a highly organized, empathetic leader to help us manage "process debt," streamline cross-functional workflows, and build an environment where our engineers can do their best work. You won't just be managing a team; you will be optimizing how we build.

 


WHAT YOU’LL DO

Drive Efficiency: The title line, #1 most important responsibility for a successful Engineering Manager at Clair is overseeing and driving the efficiency and output of the team. We want the whole team shipping product at a strong pace, while keeping a slim team as compared to Clair’s ambitions. Even though you’ll have many direct reports, you’ll want to have a strong relationship with the full tech team to drive the output holistically.

Ways you’ll drive the efficiency:

 - Lead & Mentor: Directly manage, coach, and grow a team of high-performing engineers. You will conduct impactful 1:1s, guide career development, and ensure your team feels valued and recognized.

 - Drive Cross-Functional Alignment: Observe interactions between Engineering, Product, Design, Data, Care, and more, looking for inefficiencies and guiding the teams to be better able to align themselves. You will ensure context is shared transparently and that decisions don't get lost in silos.

 - Tame the Chaos: Bring exceptional organizational skills to our engineering operations. You will identify bottlenecks, improve documentation standards (like PRDs), and create clear, lightweight processes that speed us up rather than slow us down.

 - Protect the Team: Actively monitor team workloads to ensure we are delivering high-quality work sustainably, guarding against burnout and maintaining healthy boundaries.

 - Champion Innovation: Encourage your team to experiment with AI and new tooling to solve complex problems more efficiently. Advocate for time to be invested into Engineer-led initiatives that may not be part of the core product roadmaps.

 - Bring in Talent: You’ll be a major part of hiring for the Clair Eng team. Whether finding bright and talented mid-to-senior level developers, or assisting in sourcing and vetting Staff+ level engineers, you will play a critical part in the process. We’re always aiming to level ourselves up, both from a technical perspective as well as cultural.




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Experience: 3–5+ years of direct engineering management experience, ideally leading teams through a period of rapid growth in a series B or C startup.

 - Startup DNA: You have thrived in a scaling startup environment. You are comfortable with ambiguity, shifting priorities, and building the airplane while flying it.

 - Highly Organized: You are a systems thinker. You naturally look for ways to centralize information, clean up messy workflows, and make executing work easier for everyone around you. You are diligent at tracking the ongoing work across the org and obsess over making sure stakeholders are aware of status and timelines.

 - Technical Credibility: You have a strong background in software engineering. While you won't be coding every day, you possess the technical chops to guide architectural decisions, push back when necessary, and earn the respect of your team.

 - Empathetic Leadership: You care deeply about the

source_jobs.title: Partnerships Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about


ABOUT THE ROLE

Clair's business is built on deep, strategic integrations with the platforms that power how Americans work and get paid. As a Partnerships Lead, you'll own and drive some of our most important partner relationships — acting as a voice in how we grow, deepen, and scale those integrations over time.

This is a high-impact individual contributor role. You'll work directly with Clair's partnerships and growth leadership to shape partnership strategy, own a portfolio of key accounts, and serve as the primary point of contact for partners at all levels — including executive stakeholders. You'll bring both the strategic thinking to identify what's possible and the operational follow-through to make it happen.

If you're someone who thrives on thinking commercially, bringing clarity to complex situations, building relationships, and driving initiatives forward with a high degree of ownership, you'll be right at home in this role.




WHAT YOU'LL DO

Drive Partner Growth & Expansion Think commercially about each partnership. Identify whitespace opportunities, propose new ways to expand the relationship, and work cross-functionally to bring those opportunities to life. You'll be expected to move the needle on partner-driven revenue and usership growth.

Own Partner Relationships Serve as the primary owner of a portfolio of high-value platform partnerships. Build deep, trust-based relationships with key stakeholders — including executive-level contacts — and proactively identify opportunities to grow and deepen each partnership over time.

Lead Partner Onboarding & Launch Own the end-to-end onboarding process for new partners — setting the tone for the relationship from day one, coordinating across Product, Engineering, and Operations, and ensuring a smooth and well-managed go-live.

Own Partner Performance Track, analyze, and communicate partner performance across key metrics — adoption rates, user activity, revenue impact — and translate that data into clear, compelling narratives. Lead QBRs and business reviews that demonstrate value and drive forward the strategic agenda of each partnership.

Represent the Partner Internally Be the voice of the partner at Clair. Surface product feedback, flag integration issues, and advocate clearly and persuasively across Product, Engineering, and Leadership to ensure partner needs are prioritized appropriately.




WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4–6 years of experience in product management, partnerships, consulting, account management, business development, or a related field — with a proven track record of owning high-value external relationships and driving commercial outcomes.

 - Strong executive presence — you're comfortable engaging and presenting to senior stakeholders at partner organizations and can represent Clair confidently and credibly in those conversations.

 - Product and Commercial mindset — you're comfortable navigating conversations that span business, technical, and product considerations. You can collaborate with Product teams, influence implementation decisions, and help partners understand the value and tradeoffs of different solutions.

 - Data literate — you can synthesize and tell a compelling story with partner performance data using Excel, Google Sheets, or similar tools. (know how to make a good deck)

 - Highly organized and operationally sharp — you manag

source_jobs.title: Product Data Scientist
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about



About the Role

As a Product Data Scientist at Clair, you’ll own the experimentation and analytics layer that drives our product and underwriting decisions. You’ll play a critical role in shaping how we balance growth, risk, and user experience by designing experiments, defining success metrics, and translating data into actionable product strategy.

This role sits at the intersection of product, finance, and risk. You’ll act as the central owner of all A/B testing at Clair — from underwriting experiments (e.g., advance limits, accrual velocity) to product surface testing that impacts user behavior and downstream credit outcomes. Beyond experimentation, you’ll define how we measure success, build forecasting frameworks, and ensure that product decisions are grounded in strong unit economics.

We’re looking for someone with strong business intuition, deep expertise in experimentation and statistics, and the ability to translate complex data into clear strategic recommendations. This is less about building machine learning models and more about driving decision-making through rigorous analysis, experimentation, and cross-functional influence.



Key Responsibilities

 - Own and manage Clair’s experimentation ecosystem, including the design, execution, and analysis of A/B tests across underwriting and product experiences.

 - Translate model outputs (e.g., risk scores) into actionable business decisions, such as approval thresholds and lending strategies.

 - Serve as the single source of truth for all experiments, ensuring consistency, rigor, and proper interpretation of results across the organization.

 - Design experiments that evaluate key levers such as credit limits, accrual mechanisms, and pricing, and quantify their impact on both growth and risk metrics.

 - Define and standardize core product and financial metrics, including how we measure model impact, user behavior, and unit economics.

 - Partner closely with Product, Finance, Risk, and Engineering teams to align on strategy, evaluate trade-offs, and inform decision-making.

 - Act as a strategic advisor, helping stakeholders understand the implications of experiments and guiding data-driven product development.




MINIMUM QUALIFICATIONS

 - 5+ years of experience in data science, product analytics, or a related analytical role.

 - Strong foundation in statistics and experimental design, including A/B testing, causal inference, and hypothesis testing.

 - Proven experience owning end-to-end experimentation programs and influencing product decisions through data.

 - Strong SQL skills and experience working with large datasets.

 - Demonstrated ability to translate complex analyses into clear business insights and recommendations.

 - Experience working cross-functionally with Product, Finance, or Strategy teams in a fast-paced environment.

 - Strong business intuition and ability to think in terms of trade-offs, unit economics, and growth vs. risk.




PREFERRED QUALIFICATIONS

 - Experience in fintech, lending, or credit-related products.

 - Familiarity with underwriting concepts such as risk scoring, approval strategies, and loss modeling.

 - Experience building forecasting models for business or financial metrics.

 - Proficiency in Python or R for data analysis.

 - Experience with experimentation platforms and statistical tooling.



Additional Details

L

source_jobs.title: Regulatory and Product Counsel
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about


ABOUT THE ROLE

We are looking for a Regulatory and Product Counsel to join our legal and compliance team. This is a high-impact role for a lawyer who wants to be at the center of a fast-growing fintech—advising on product development, supporting our compliance management system (CMS), and helping navigate an evolving regulatory landscape. You will work directly with our General Counsel and partner cross-functionally with Product, Marketing, and Operations.




WHAT YOU’LL DO

 - Serve as a primary legal advisor on product counseling matters, providing practical regulatory guidance on new product features, partnerships, and go-to-market strategies

 - Advise on consumer financial protection laws, including UDAAP, fair lending, state licensing requirements (lending, money transmission), and other applicable federal and state regulations

 - Support all aspects of the compliance management system (CMS), including policies and procedures, training, monitoring, and corrective action

 - Review and approve customer-facing materials and marketing collateral for regulatory accuracy and required disclosures

 - Assist with third-party and vendor risk management, including review of vendor contracts and oversight programs

 - Support licensing portfolio management, including tracking deadlines, coordinating renewals, and preparing regulatory filings

 - Assist with internal and external audits and regulatory examinations, including document preparation and response coordination

 - Liaise with our Partner Bank on compliance-related matters and communicate effectively with Partner Bank stakeholders

 - Monitor regulatory developments and translate legal changes into actionable guidance for internal teams




WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - J.D. and active bar admission in at least one U.S. jurisdiction

 - 3+ years of regulatory legal experience, including time at a law firm, regulatory agency, financial institution, or in-house at a fintech or financial services company

 - Substantive knowledge of consumer financial protection laws and regulations (e.g., TILA, EFTA, ECOA, UDAAP, state lending laws)

 - Experience advising on product or business initiatives in a regulated environment

 - Familiarity with compliance management systems and their core components

 - Excellent written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to translate complex legal concepts into clear, practical guidance

 - Proactive, solutions-oriented mindset with the ability to manage competing priorities in a fast-paced environment

 - Bonus: Experience with state money transmission or lending licensing, partner bank relationships, or third-party risk management frameworks




ADDITIONAL DETAILS

Location: While we are based in NYC, we are also open to remote candidates for this role who are based in US only. If you are based in NYC, you'll be expected to be in the office 3- Days a week.

Compensation: The annual base salary for this role is $160,000 and $180,000. The base pay for this role is determined using many factors, such as education, skills and experience and is reflective of Clair Series stage and size. Base pay is only one part of Clair’s competitive total compensation package which includes equity, benefits and additional perks. The base pay range is subject to change and may be modified in the future.



Clair will only contact candidates from @get

source_jobs.title: Accounting Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about




WHO WE’RE LOOKING FOR

We’re seeking a detailed-oriented Accounting Manager to join the growing Accounting team. Reporting to the Controller, you’ll take ownership of accounting operations — procure to pay, record to report, and month-end close. You will be a critical and key partner on a lean team to ensure our financial reporting is timely, accurate, and compliant with relevant accounting standards.



Our ideal candidate is meticulous, process-driven, and able to thrive in a fast-paced startup environment. You should be comfortable juggling multiple priorities while maintaining exceptional attention to detail. This role requires someone with a proven track record of establishing and improving accounting procedures at scale, while consistently meeting deadlines within a high-growth setting.



This role is open to remote candidates, preferably in the U.S, Eastern timezone.




CORE RESPONSIBILITIES

Month-End Close Process

 - Execute core components of the monthly close process, ensuring accuracy and timeliness

 - Prepare and post journal entries, account reconciliations, and supporting schedules

 - Review balance sheet accounts to ensure accuracy and completeness

 - Analyze financial statement fluctuations and variances to prior periods

 - Utilize and manage offshore resources for efficiency

 - Streamlining and improving the speed and accuracy of the overall close process



Financial Reporting & Compliance

 - Prepare the monthly, quarterly, and annual financial statements (includes intercompany and consolidations)

 - Assist in maintaining Month-end close review decks

 - Support audit requirements by preparing documentation and responding to inquiries

 - Help maintain compliance with GAAP and other relevant accounting standards

 - Work with external tax preparers by providing supporting schedules for tax filings and other requests, as well as assistance with other regulatory requirements

 - Document accounting policies and procedures to ensure consistent application

 - Assist with other ad-hoc projects that may include preparing technical accounting memos



Accounts Payable

 - Review and process vendor invoices, ensuring proper coding and approvals

 - Own and ensure adoption of the procurement policy

 - Ensure procurement tool approvals are provided timely

 - Ensure vendor payments and reimbursements are coded appropriately



Cash Management & Treasury

 - Assist Finance with cash flow forecasting and monitoring

 - Reconcile bank accounts on a regular basis

 - Process and record wire transfers and other banking transactions

 - Support treasury operations, as needed



Systems & Process Improvement

 - Identify opportunities to automate and streamline processes

 - Support implementation and optimization of new tools (e.g., ERPs)

 - Create and maintain documentation for accounting processes and controls

 - Assist in developing scalable processes to support company growth




REQUIRED EXPERIENCE & QUALIFICATIONS

 - Bachelor’s degree or higher in Accounting (required)

 - Active CPA (preferred)

 - 5+ years of progressive accounting experience, including Big Four (or large public accounting) audit experience

 - In-house accounting experience as a lead/owner at a growth-stage company on a lean team

 - A track record of owning balance-sheet accounting independently and supporting or leading external

source_jobs.title: Marketing Events & Communications Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about

About the Role

We're looking for someone who knows that the best partnerships often start with a great conversation, and that the right story in the right outlet can open doors that no sales deck can.

As our Marketing Events & Communications Manager, you know how to get things done: you can own the logistics of a Tier 1 executive dinner, keep a PR agency on track, and make sure nothing falls through the cracks between a media pitch and a conference booth.

You'll work closely with Clair's B2B Marketing lead to execute partner-facing and prospect-facing events and support day-to-day PR activities. You won't be setting the overall strategy from scratch—you'll be the person who makes sure it actually happens, on time and with strong attention to detail.

This is an ideal role for someone who is organized, proactive, and comfortable switching between event logistics, media coordination, and agency management in the same week.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


EVENT STRATEGY & PLANNING

 - Identify conferences, sponsorship opportunities, events, podcasts, and industry gatherings that align with Clair's go-to-market strategy.

 - Develop an annual and quarterly event calendar focused on driving partner and prospect engagement.

 - Evaluate event ROI and make recommendations on where Clair should invest time and resources.


EVENT EXECUTION

 - Own end-to-end event planning and logistics, including vendor management, contracts, budgets, registrations, travel coordination, venue selection, and on-site execution.

 - Coordinate executive dinners, customer appreciation events, prospect roundtables, and partner gatherings from start to finish.

 - Lead Clair's operational participation in industry conferences, including HR Tech, Money20/20, Fintech Meetup, and other relevant events.

 - Support New York Tech Week programming and local community-building initiatives.

PR & External Communications

 - Serve as the operational day-to-day liaison with Clair's PR agency—tracking open items, following up on deliverables, and flagging blockers to the Head of B2B Marketing

 - Maintain and update Clair's media list of target journalists, newsletter writers, podcast hosts, and analysts covering fintech, HR tech, payroll, and embedded finance

 - Draft and send media pitches under the direction of the Head of B2B Marketing; coordinate logistics for executive interviews and press calls

 - Prepare briefing documents for executive media appearances, including journalist background research, key talking points, and logistics

 - Monitor media coverage and flag relevant press mentions, competitive coverage, and inbound inquiries

 - Support award nomination submissions—gathering materials, drafting responses, and managing deadlines

 - Coordinate logistics for speaking opportunities and podcast appearances, including scheduling, prep materials, and follow-up

Revenue & Partnership Support

 - Partner closely with Sales, Partnerships, Marketing, and Leadership teams to ensure events and communications efforts generate meaningful business outcomes

 - Help identify target attendees and coordinate outreach efforts before, during, and after events

 - Work with leadership to maximize networking opportunities and strategic introductions

Measurement & Operations

 - Track event performance and maintain reporting on attendance, meetings generated, pipeline influenced,

source_jobs.title: Executive Assistant
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT CLAIR

If you can send your friends money in seconds, why does it still take your employer two weeks to send your paycheck?

At Clair, we are on a mission to create financial freedom for America's workers by giving them a digital banking platform that allows them to get paid as soon as they clock out of work. But we're not just another digital bank or on-demand pay provider. We meet Americans at their place of work by embedding our products within the scheduling, workforce management, and payroll apps they already use every day.

Learn more about us at getclair.com/about http://getclair.com/about


ABOUT THE ROLE

We're looking for an exceptional Executive Assistant to support our CEO. This is not a traditional calendar-management role.

Our CEO is deeply involved in founder-led strategic partnerships, fundraising conversations, and company leadership. Success in this role requires someone who can operate as the CEO's force multiplier—keeping priorities moving, creating leverage, and ensuring critical conversations don't fall through the cracks.

You'll spend a significant portion of your time supporting new partnerships and existing partnership efforts by coordinating meetings, managing follow-ups, tracking outreach, and helping maintain momentum across a wide range of external stakeholders. You'll need to be comfortable applying pressure when necessary, tracking down information, and ensuring the right people are moving with urgency.

If you're highly organized, relentlessly detail-oriented, proactive, and energized by helping a company grow, we'd love to meet you.




WHAT YOU'LL DO


EXECUTIVE SUPPORT

 - Manage and optimize the CEO's complex calendar and priorities

 - Coordinate internal and external meetings across multiple stakeholders and time zones

 - Prepare meeting materials, agendas, and briefing documents

 - Help ensure action items and commitments are captured and completed

 - Serve as a trusted gatekeeper and strategic partner to the CEO

 - Provide ad-hoc personal administrative support and lifestyle management


FOUNDER-LED PARTNERSHIP SUPPORT

 - Coordinate meetings with prospective customers, partners, investors, and advisors

 - Drive scheduling across busy executives and external stakeholders

 - Track meeting requests, follow-ups, and next steps

 - Partner closely with Sales, Partnerships, and Leadership teams to keep opportunities moving

 - Help maintain CRM hygiene and sales process discipline

 - Proactively chase down information and remove bottlenecks that could slow deals


OPERATIONAL EXCELLENCE

 - Create systems and processes that help the CEO operate more efficiently

 - Maintain organization across key initiatives and strategic projects

 - Anticipate needs before they become urgent

 - Help prepare for board meetings, leadership meetings, offsites, and customer events

 - Act as a true partner and life-optimizer, managing everything from complex business calendars to occasional personal scheduling and logistics so the Executive can operate at peak productivity
   
   


WHAT WE'RE LOOKING FOR

 - 4+ years of Executive Assistant experience supporting senior executives

 - Previous experience supporting a Chief Revenue Officer, Head of Sales, Founder-led sales organization, or similarly customer-facing executive is required

 - Exceptional organizational skills and attention to detail

 - Strong written and verbal communication skills

 - Comfortable operating in a fast-paced startup environment with shifting priorities

 - Highly proactive and able to work independently

 - Ability to influence without authority and keep people accountable to deadlines

 - Strong judgment and discretion when handling sensitive information

 - Experience coordinating complex calendars and external stakeholder meetings

 - Willingness to occasionally travel and support company events as needed
   
   


YOU'LL THRIVE HERE IF YOU...

 - Love being the person who knows what's happening before everyone e

notes: Fintech startup with a mix of tech and non-tech roles
